mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling from already-low levels, the discount-rate channel dominates: lower short rates reduce equity valuation pressure and support higher S&P 500 prices over the next month. This mechanism is falsified if rate cuts are not followed by positive 20-day index returns more often than the unconditional base rate.
failure_condition: If periods with a declining FEDFUNDS series do not produce a higher-than-baseline share of positive 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the rolling 2-year rank IC stays below 0.03, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a stressed labor-market threshold indicates weakening household income expectations and higher recession risk, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if elevated unemployment does not coincide with negative forward equity returns after the trigger date.
failure_condition: Reject if, after UNRATE crosses above 9.5%, the next-20-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 60% of cases or if the average forward return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly drop in RSAFS signals weakening consumer and retail demand, which should lead analysts to lower earnings expectations and reduce S&P 500 returns over the following month. The effect is falsified if demand contractions are followed by positive equity performance more often than not.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS falls by more than 1.0% month-over-month
failure_condition: If months with RSAFS declines greater than 1.0% are not associated with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns and the hit rate is not below 50%, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO and PAYEMS both improve at the same time, the economy is expanding through broad production and hiring gains, which should support cyclical earnings and lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The interaction matters because factory output and payroll growth jointly confirm real activity rather than a single noisy release.
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive monthly changes in INDPRO and PAYEMS do not improve 60-day forward S&P 500 returns relative to single-series cases, or if the combined signal has no incremental predictive value over either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in HOUST after a prior slump signals stabilization in housing demand and construction activity, which feeds into employment, credit creation, and cyclically sensitive earnings. This should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month housing-start rebounds are not followed by above-baseline positive 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the average post-trigger return is non-positive.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a combination of rising FEDFUNDS and rising CPIAUCSL indicates inflation pressure is forcing policy restraint and higher discount rates, which should push S&P 500 returns lower over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if tighter policy is not accompanied by weaker equity performance.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous increases in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL do not produce lower 20-day S&P 500 returns than either series alone, or if the signal flips positive during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A declining PAYEMS trend during a weak labor market regime confirms payroll deterioration, which should reduce risk appetite and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week. The effect is strongest when unemployment is already high because labor softness then signals broader recession risk.
failure_condition: Reject if declining PAYEMS combined with elevated UNRATE does not produce negative 5-day S&P 500 returns more often than the unconditional rate, or if the interaction term is not statistically distinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A positive GDP surprise in GDPC1 during recovery conditions signals stronger aggregate demand and improving earnings prospects, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism is falsified if stronger quarterly GDP readings do not translate into positive medium-horizon equity performance.
failure_condition: Reject if quarters with accelerating GDPC1 do not show higher 60-day forward S&P 500 returns than quarters without acceleration, or if the effect disappears in rolling out-of-sample tests.
